EQP-1: Provides a finite natural number function f defined over the domain {0..n−1}.
EQP-2: Provides n as the size of the function’s domain.
FUN-1: The system must determine an index p such that f(p) = max(ran(f)).
FUN-2: In the abstract model maxi_m0, the final condition requires p ∈ dom(f) and f(p) = max(ran(f)).
FUN-3: In the refined model maxi_m1, the system introduces a Boolean variable searching to indicate that the maximum has not yet been located. The search interval is delimited by two indices p and q.
FUN-4: Initialization sets p = 0 and q = n−1, covering the entire domain and starting the search with searching = true.
FUN-5: If searching is true, and p < q with f(p) < f(q), the algorithm increments p to shrink the interval from the left.
FUN-6: If searching is true, and p < q with f(p) ≥ f(q), the algorithm decrements q to shrink the interval from the right.
FUN-7: When p ≥ q, searching becomes false, marking that the maximum index has been found.
SAF-1: As long as searching is true, the maximum of f is always contained within the current interval [p..q].
SAF-2: The interval [p..q] is never empty until termination, guaranteeing the maximum is not lost.
SAF-3: The final guard p≥q implies the abstract condition that p is the index of the maximum.
DEG-1: If n=0 (empty domain), no maximum can be defined and the algorithm cannot proceed.
DEG-2: If n=1, initialization directly satisfies the final condition (p=q=0).
